@startuml
actor User
participant "SearchWindowUI" as SearchWindowUI
participant "SearchOnAnotherInstanceController" as SearchOnAnotherInstanceController
participant NetworkFactory
participant UDP_ClientImpl1 as UDPClientImpl_03 {
    participant Thread
}
participant TCP_ClientImpl2 as TCPClientImpl_03 {
    participant SpreadCheetah
}
participant UDPCientImpI_04 as UDPClientImpl_05
participant TCPClentlmpl_06 as TCPClientImpl_07
participant SearchWindowController

User ->> SearchWindowUI : start searching instances
activate SearchWindowUI
SearchWindowUI->>+SearchOnAnotherInstanceController:create(address) 
note right of SearchOnAnotherInstanceController: Obj = getUDPClientImpl(availableInstances)\ncreate()\ngetInfo(destination)\nclose() 

group create(obj);\njoin(); getInfo(dest);\nclose() #LightBlue;Text:black;line:green;text align center  
  alt available instance found? then   
     loop while not finished do    
       par parallel execution      
         rect rgb(200, 89 ,12 )#FFCCBB       
           note left:this is colored          
             thread.start(new Runnable(){public void run(){try{Thread.sleep((long)(Math.random()*1E+D));System.out.println("Hello from "+getName());}}});        
          end         
        else sequential part here           
      End Parallel Block Here!!!!!              
   END LOOP OVER ALL THREADS TO BE STARTED AT ONCE !!!!!!!!                
else NO AVAILABLE INSTANCE FOUND SO CREATE NEW ONE INSTEAD !!!     
end group closing bracket for this whole block above which includes both alternatives together under same parent node i.e., either/or condition check first before proceeding further down path depending upon outcome/result obtained during evaluation phase itself! So now we have two separate branches emerging out based on whether any existing server was detected earlier step one already completed successfully without errors occurring anywhere along way so far thus making sense logically speaking anyway...  

@enduml
